Fuel Supply Stable For Now Despite Global Oil Turbulence, Says Department - African Farming ### African Farming Agri-Development Imbizo 2026 – Meet the speakers: Dr Lerato Matsaunyane. ### African Farming Agri-Development Imbizo 2026 – Meet the speakers: Dr Lerato Matsaunyane. *The Department of Mineral and Petroleum Resources* *has reassured South Africans that the country’s fuel supply remains stable in the short term, despite rising volatility on global energy markets linked to escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.*. The department warned that sustained increases in global oil prices, coupled with exchange rate fluctuations, are likely to translate into higher domestic fuel prices in the months ahead, adding further pressure on farmers and agribusinesses already grappling with rising input costs,” said the department. Established in 2020, African Farming aims to support black commercial farming in South Africa by providing informative and inspiring content and creating communication and education channels to help farmers develop and grow. Its initiatives include African Farming information days and workshops, which empower farmers – whether new or experienced – to build profitable and sustainable farming enterprises. https://www.africanfarming.com/2026/03/21/fuel-supply-stable-for-now-despite-global-oil-turbulence-says-department/
